diagrams.title: Scheduler Hierarchy in Operating System
diagrams.description: Shows three levels of schedulers: Long-term (job pool to ready queue), Medium-term (memory to secondary storage), Short-term (ready queue to CPU)
diagrams.purpose: Understanding the three-tier scheduling architecture and data flow between components

diagrams.title: Context Switching Process
diagrams.description: Illustrates how CPU switches from one process to another by saving and restoring process states
diagrams.purpose: Visualizing the steps involved in context switching between processes

diagrams.title: Gantt Chart - FCFS vs SJF vs Round Robin
diagrams.description: Comparative visualization of three scheduling algorithms on same set of processes
elements: Timeline showing execution order for each algorithm
Waiting time and turnaround time comparisons
diagrams.purpose: Comparing how different algorithms schedule the same set of processes and their impact on performance metrics
